title: [GPIO Interface] Verify XX_PROCHOT_N input pin assertion
description:
(Sections with * are mandatory. You must fill them in, or say "Not Applicable" or "N/A" if you have seriously considered it and determined there's nothing to say. Remove these instructions and the explanatory text in each section as you fill things in.)


Flow *

XX_PROCHOT_N is single input pin at the package level. Platform uses the input Prochot mode to indicate power capping due to thermal reasons and when asserted the CPU needs to go the Prochot Reponse Power as programmed by customer.


flip header on board as below:

The board files you can find here: https://wiki.ith.intel.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=3637512760#JC2POB-DesignCollaterals

 

Depend of fab that you have is the version of layout. 

 

Here you can find TOPs information https://wiki.ith.intel.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=3943861927

 

There you can find more information about thermal location. https://wiki.ith.intel.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=3943862324&preview=/3943862324/4085091191/OKS-AP%20JNC2%20-%20Errors%20and%20Interrupts%20Visual%20Aid%20-%20REV_03.pdf 


then pkgc therm satus reads prochot and ratios are goto pm
